completedItems: Habit completion persists across app reloads
Anchor marked complete when all its habits are done
Flame grows with each completed anchor
Streak state reconciles by date on reopen
Flame shrinks (never resets) on a missed day
Streak/flame value stored offline in local storage
Anchor-time notifications scheduled with in-voice copy
Onboarding enforces exactly three habit picks
Weekly reflection screen composes the current week
Free tier limits to 1 anchor and 2 habits
notes: No open-issues list was provided this round, so work went straight to the 11 roadmap items. Key changes: store/habits.ts now keeps a per-day `history` ledger (dateKey -> habitId -> bool) instead of a single flat `completed` map, so today's checks persist across reloads and past days feed the reflection screen. `flameHeat` (0-1, persisted) replaces the old streak/16 heuristic as the Flame's driver: toggling the last habit in an anchor grows it by a notch, unchecking reverts it, and it's floored so it never reaches zero once lit. lib/reconcile.ts is the single-file reconciliation module (per item 4/5's exact wording) — pure `reconcileStreak()` compares lastActiveDate to today, dims the flame by a fixed penalty per fully-missed calendar day, and floors above zero. store/habits.ts#reconcile() calls it on every date rollover (invoked once per app open from app/_layout.tsx after habits-store hydration) and also derives the traditional day-streak counter from whether *yesterday* was fully kept in history — that counter can hit zero on a miss (like any habit app), which is the point: the flame staying lit through it is the differentiator, not another number that also survives. Anchor-complete visual state (Badge 'Kept', success-tinted border/ring/glyph) and free-tier locking (only the first anchor + first two habits are interactive; everything else shows a lock icon + 'Pro' badge and routes to /paywall) were added to the Home screen. The Reflection tab now computes the current Mon-Sun week's ember-strip and 'X / Y anchors kept' stat from real stored history (counting only fully-elapsed days so an in-progress today is never counted as a slip), falls back to DESIGN.md's brand-new-account empty state when there's no elapsed history yet, and generates its slip-count summary and steadiest-anchor pull-quote dynamically instead of hardcoding '18/21... three slips'. Added lib/notifications.ts on top of the newly-installed expo-notifications: three DAILY-repeating local notifications at the anchor windows with the exact in-voice copy from DESIGN.md §7 ('morning anchor — two small things.', etc.), plus a re-engagement nudge fired when reconciliation finds a missed day. Every call is guarded behind `Platform.OS === 'web'` (no-op there) and the package's web-fallback modules are JS-only no-ops, so `expo export --platform web` is unaffected; verified via `npx expo-doctor` (18/18) and by reading the web-fallback sources directly, since builds aren't run from this session per instructions. Onboarding's preset habit library (12 real tiny habits across the three anchors, tap-to-select, no free-text field) and its exactly-three-picks enforcement (CTA disabled until 3, 4th tap blocked) were already correct from a prior round and needed no changes beyond wiring notification scheduling into its finish step. Demo-mode history is seeded for the days before 'today' in the current week (a full day, a midday slip, a full day, an evening slip, a full day) marking every habit in the candidate library per anchor (not just the pre-onboarding defaults), so the seed reads correctly regardless of which three habits the user ends up picking in onboarding. `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean throughout.
